items.title: Shoriful four-for, Tanzid 94 guide Bangladesh to consolation win
items.summary: A 151-run partnership between Tanzid Hasan and Soumya Sarkar sealed an easy chase against a second-choice Zimbabwe bowling attack

items.title: Shoriful, openers shine as Tigers earn consolation victory
items.summary: Having already conceded the series after losing the opening two matches, the visitors produced a clinical all-round display to finish the series on a positive note.

items.title: West Indies desperate for ODI upswing with World Cup qualification on the line
items.summary: For New Zealand, meanwhile, this series offers a chance to get back to winning ways after a defeat to Bangladesh in their last ODI assignment

items.title: Bangladesh bowl; Raza leads Zimbabwe in Ngarava's absence
items.summary: Bangladesh left out fast bowler Nahid Rana and legspinner Rishad Hossain while Zimbabwe rested Blessing Muzarabani and Newman Nyamhuri

items.title: Bangladesh bowl with two changes in 3rd ODI
items.summary: Bangladesh will bowl first in the third and final ODI against Zimbabwe in Harare today after making two changes to their playing XI for the dead rubber.

items.title: Mustafizur Rahman ruled out for four weeks with hamstring and knee injuries
items.summary: Seamer will travel to Birmingham for rehabilitation after being ruled out of Zimbabwe tour

items.title: Zimbabwe eye 3-0 sweep as Bangladesh seek consolation
items.summary: Zimbabwe haven't completed a clean sweep of Bangladesh in any format since 2001

items.title: Tigers hit new low in Zimbabwe amid World Cup fever
items.summary: "I am not fully sure whether they were a bit complacent after going there following wins against some formidable opponents. Another important factor, I think, was that the conditions were a bit challenging and they could not figure out how to adjust to them," chief selector Habibul Bashar explained.

items.title: Curran and Ngarava seal Zimbabwe's stunning series win
items.summary: Bangladesh lost 7 for 65 in the chase of 248 to lose the second ODI and the series

items.title: Bangladesh suffer 13-run defeat to concede ODI series to Zimbabwe
items.summary: The visitors lost their last four wickets for just 15 runs and were bowled out for 234 in 48.1 overs.

items.title: Litton Das ruled out of ODI series against Zimbabwe with calf injury
items.summary: Parvez Hossain Emon has been named Litton's replacement for the second and third ODIs

items.title: Bangladesh look to overturn batting woes as Zimbabwe eye series win
items.summary: Bangladesh's tour has been marred by collapses so far and they can't afford another one

items.title: Shoriful returns as Bangladesh bowl against unchanged Zimbabwe
items.summary: Visitors need a victory to level the series while hosts eye a series win today

items.title: Bangladesh opt to field in must-win second ODI against Zimbabwe
items.summary: Bangladesh won the toss and opted to field first in the second of their three-match ODI series against hosts Zimbabwe in Harare today.

items.title: Nyamhuri, Shumba in Zimbabwe T20I squad against Bangladesh
items.summary: Cremer was ruled out with a left-arm fracture, while Taylor and Tony Munyonga are the other changes from the World Cup squad
